concerns.text: Task T013 mandates fetching data from 'numenta/NAB' or 'specific FCG repos', which violates FR-001 and Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) by failing to mandate the canonical sources (NASA Fracture Control Database, NIST Materials Data Repository) explicitly required by the spec. This introduces a silent relaxation of the data source constraint.

Task T029 specifies using 'ruptures' for 'change-point detection on residuals', which is a segmentation methodology. This contradicts FR-006 and FR-007 which explicitly mandate 'continuous interaction analysis (e.g., varying coefficient models)'. The task delivers a different statistical mechanism than the spec requires, violating the constraint on the analysis method.

Task T034 phrases the generalizability test as 'if present' regarding distinct alloy families (e.g., Titanium). This violates the Edge Case requirement in spec.md which states the system 'MUST evaluate the model on this distinct subset'. The task allows the requirement to be skipped if the data lacks diversity, whereas the spec mandates the test (implying a need for a separate validation set or a halt if impossible).

Task T016 instructs calculating a 'p-value against null model' without defining the null model. FR-002 and US-1 Acceptance Scenario 2 require verifying significance against a null model to confirm the baseline is valid. Without explicitly defining the null model (e.g., intercept-only/horizontal line) in the task, the deliverable risks validating the wrong hypothesis, failing the acceptance criterion.
